sourceMovie.title: Big Momma's House
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Big Momma's House (2000) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Red Notice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Big Momma's House clicked for you, Red Notice (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Big Momma's House?
faq.answer: Try Red Notice, 2 Guns, White Chicks—each shares DNA with Big Momma's House while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Big Momma's House?
faq.answer: Big Momma's House (2000) is best described as Crime, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Big Momma's House part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Big Momma's House Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
